_meta.description: D6 fixtures for hermes / tool-rendering-default-catchall. SERVER-SIDE backend tools (Hermes `hermes-showcase` toolset, integrations/hermes/showcase_tools.py), 1:1 with langgraph-python; rendered by CopilotKit's BUILT-IN default tool card (useDefaultRenderTool()). The fixture only makes the agent EMIT the tool call (leg 1, gated on toolName); Hermes runs the REAL handler in-loop (result via TOOL_CALL_RESULT), then the narration leg (gated on toolCallId, ordered first) fires. The tool RESULT comes from the server handler, NOT the fixture. The built-in renderer paints [data-testid='copilot-tool-render'][data-tool-name='<tool>'] with a status pill. Two-leg toolCallId pattern (2nd leg above 1st). D5 probe prompt: 'forecast for Tokyo' (disjoint from the custom-catchall probe's 'Forecast Tokyo through the wildcard renderer' so aimock's substring matcher cannot cross-route). Pills (verbatim from suggestions.ts): "What's the weather in San Francisco?", "Find flights from SFO to JFK.", "Roll a 20-sided die.", "Chain a few tools in this single turn: ...".

fixtures._comment: Chain tools — follow-up after all 3 tools ran (last chain toolCallId).

match.userMessage: Chain a few tools in this single turn

response.content: Done — Tokyo is sunny, three flights found, and the d20 came up 11.

What's the weather in San Francisco?

response.content: San Francisco is currently 68°F and sunny with light winds.

match.userMessage: What's the weather in San Francisco?
Find flights from SFO to JFK.

response.content: Three flights from SFO to JFK — United UA231, Delta DL412, and JetBlue B6722.

match.userMessage: Find flights from SFO to JFK.

fixtures._comment: Roll a d20 — 5 sequential rolls [7,14,3,19,20], chained by toolCallId.

response.content: Rolled the d20 five times — landed on 20 on the final roll.

fixtures._comment: D5 probe: 'forecast for Tokyo' — follow-up content after get_weather ran. MUST come before the tool-emitting fixture. The built-in default renderer renders [data-testid='copilot-tool-render'][data-tool-name='get_weather'] with a status pill.

response.content: Tokyo is 68°F and sunny — the default catchall renderer displayed the tool card above.

fixtures._comment: D5 probe: 'forecast for Tokyo' — first leg: emit get_weather that the built-in default catchall renders.
